evals.prompt: Use $repo-bug-audit at /path/to/repo-bug-audit to run a deep static Bug audit on a parent directory that contains 20 Git repositories, and produce one final handoff package.
must_do: Treat the parent directory as a repo-group root even if the parent is not a Git checkout.
Run discover_repositories.py and freeze the roster before Bug hunting.
Generate LLM-targeted scan patterns after agent exploration, run run_high_recall_scan.py --patterns-file.
Triage representative search seeds into shard seed_triage before declaring candidate counts.
Create one repo shard per discovered repo, or record a concrete batching reason for tiny coupled repos.
Fill each non-trivial shard with a risk surface map and hypothesis loops before final packaging.
Run validate_bug_package.py with --validate-shards-only before final README, HTML, overview image, or final knowledge packaging.
Generate bug-audit-report.html for the final handoff package.
Use first-pass or focused unless deep-complete is supported by per-repo shard evidence.
must_not_do: Analyze only the first few repositories and still present the package as complete.
Use cross-repo grep as a substitute for repo-local shard exploration.
Treat pattern searches as repo understanding without surface maps and hypothesis loops.
Claim deep-complete without filled shard summaries and call-chain evidence.
success_evidence: Transcript shows discover_repositories.py before validate_bug_package.py --validate-shards-only.
Transcript shows --validate-shards-only before generate_bug_report_html.py.
Final validation exits 0 with --require-knowledge --require-html-report and --repo-root.
Shard summaries contain surface_map and hypothesis_loops evidence for non-trivial repos.
Shard summaries with search seeds contain seed_triage entries with promoted, parked, refuted, or merged outcomes.
Depth coverage states first-pass, focused, or deep-complete with matching evidence.

evals.prompt: Use $repo-bug-audit at /path/to/repo-bug-audit to finish a final handoff package for a parent directory with 20 repositories. The final findings contain submitted Bugs in only 16 repositories.
must_do: Keep quality/repository-versions.md as the complete detailed version-evidence table.
Add a simplified Analysis Scope and Version Baseline table to submit/README.md.
Show only repository, audit branch, commit, dirty/worktree status, and submitted Bug count in the README/HTML main scope table.
List every analyzed repository in README and HTML, including the four repositories with 0 submitted Bugs.
Use the HTML repository metric for analyzed repositories, not only repositories with submitted Bugs.
Keep default branch, stable candidate, candidate confidence, evidence, and notes out of the README/HTML main scope table unless a short branch-baseline exception note is needed.
must_not_do: Let the README or HTML imply that only the 16 repositories with submitted Bugs were analyzed.
Hide 0-Bug repositories in quality/repository-versions.md only.
Use the findings repo distribution as the analyzed repository count.
Overload the README/HTML main table with default branch, stable candidate, role, evidence, or notes columns.
success_evidence: README contains a scope baseline table with repository, audit branch, commit, dirty/worktree status, and submitted Bug count columns.
README names all 20 analyzed repositories and includes rows with submitted Bug count 0.
indexes/audit-scope.generated.json contains all 20 analyzed repositories and at least four zero-Bug rows.
HTML contains an analysis-scope section listing all 20 analyzed repositories.
HTML repository metric reports 20 analyzed repositories even when only 16 repositories have submitted Bugs.
validate_bug_package.py --require-html-report --require-knowledge --repo-root exits 0.

evals.prompt: Use $repo-bug-audit at /path/to/repo-bug-audit to find as many static security/auth Bugs as possible across a parent directory containing many Git repositories, then produce a final handoff package.
must_do: Treat candidate discovery as a first-class recall phase before final Bug promotion.
After initial repo-local exploration, generate LLM-targeted patterns and run run_high_recall_scan.py --patterns-file.
Convert reviewed supplemental search seeds into seed_triage outcomes before final candidate indexing.
Retain medium/low-confidence leads as candidates instead of silently dropping them.
Preserve credible P3/P4 leads as candidates or submitted Bugs instead of stopping at P1/P2.
Run a P1-P4 Priority Promotion Sweep before final handoff, using candidate priority, outcome, bug_gate, and missing_gate fields where available.
Promote every gate-complete candidate into findings/P1-P4, regardless of priority, unless the user explicitly requested critical-only scope.
Review every parked P1/P2 candidate before finalization and either promote it or document the missing Bug gate.
Create issue-family coverage from the fresh current-source scan before final promotion.
Set shard candidate_count to include leads later promoted to submitted Bugs.
Run generate_candidate_index.py before pre-package validation.
Include submit/indexes/candidates.generated.json and submit/quality/candidate-coverage.md in the final package.
must_not_do: Report only the final submitted Bug count when the prompt asks for broad discovery.
Let a low confirmed Bug count hide a much larger candidate pool.
Claim deep-complete when a high-recall scan has many seeds but only a tiny retained candidate pool.
Leave likely P1/P2 leads parked only because stronger Bugs were already submitted.
Stop after producing only P1 findings when retained P2/P3/P4 candidates have complete code evidence, trigger path, realistic failure mode, affected resource, and impact.
Leave any candidate marked bug_gate=complete unsubmitted without a merge target.
Generate final HTML before the candidate funnel is current.
success_evidence: Transcript shows generate_candidate_index.py before pre-package validate_bug_package.py.
candidate-coverage.md shows Candidate Funnel and Repository Candidate Coverage.
candidate-coverage.md shows Priority Promotion Sweep with P1-P4 candidate outcomes.
Shard summaries contain seed_triage entries for repos with high-recall search seeds.
candidates.generated.json total_candidates matches summed shard candidate_count.
Submitted findings do not exceed total candidate leads.
candidates.generated.json has zero gate_complete_unsubmitted_candidates, unless critical-only scope is explicitly recorded.

evals.prompt: Use $repo-bug-audit at /path/to/repo-bug-audit to audit a medium repository group and create the final package under the requested output directory.
must_do: Write final findings and knowledge from repo-local shard evidence.
Use scripts only for deterministic rendering, indexing, validation, or report generation.
Let validate_bug_package.py flag any late mass-generation package writer.
must_not_do: Create work/tmp/write_package.py or a similarly named script that writes final Bug records, repo profiles, depth coverage, lens coverage, and knowledge docs from memory.
Create work/scripts/build_evidence_docs.py or any audit-local script that writes multiple final Markdown deliverable families from embedded prose.
Synthesize final Markdown deliverables before shard evidence is filled.
success_evidence: No work/tmp package writer is present.
Validation output does not report generated-looking final Markdown.
Final package files cite shard evidence, real paths, and candidate records.

evals.prompt: Use $repo-bug-audit at /path/to/repo-bug-audit to run a first-time deep high-recall static Bug audit on a parent directory containing many Git repositories. There is no previous audit package.
must_do: Assume no historical package exists and do not ask for one.
Run discover_repositories.py, then after initial repo-local exploration run run_high_recall_scan.py --patterns-file against frozen repo-scan-roots.txt.
Build issue-family coverage from fresh current-source evidence before final promotion.
Cover auth, secrets, execution, deserialization/IPC, file/path, network/SSRF, TLS, frontend rendering, state integrity, async lifecycle, resource/concurrency, and deployment/supply-chain families.
Keep credible lower-confidence leads as candidates instead of silently dropping them.
Run generate_candidate_index.py and final validation before report assets.
must_not_do: Depend on a previous audit package to know what families to search.
Say a low Bug count is complete without issue-family outcomes and a visible candidate funnel.
Generate final HTML before issue-family coverage and candidate index exist.
success_evidence: Issue-family coverage states fresh/current-source scan provenance.
All declared issue families have fresh sources, outcomes, and evidence.
Candidate funnel, seed triage, and issue-family coverage explain promoted, parked, refuted, or not-applicable outcomes.
No historical baseline artifact is required for success.

evals.prompt: Use $repo-bug-audit at /path/to/repo-bug-audit to produce a final handoff report for this repository group.
must_do: Generate submit/bug-audit-report.html by default for the final handoff package.
Mention the optional audit-overview.png at kickoff without stopping the audit.
If audit-overview.png was not requested or declined, finish validation and the handoff summary first, then ask at most one yes/no follow-up question about adding it.
Record the audit-overview.png decision in submission-scope.md even when the image is omitted.
Keep repo-bug-audit, github.com/aiden0z/skills, and source=static-analysis provenance in the HTML.
must_not_do: Ask the user whether to generate the default HTML report.
Block kickoff or final validation on an unanswered audit-overview.png question.
Silently omit audit-overview.png without recording included, omitted-by-user, omitted-as-lightweight-scan, omitted-after-failure, or deferred-post-handoff.
success_evidence: HTML report exists and passes validate_bug_package.py --require-html-report.
Transcript contains no question asking whether HTML should be generated.
Overview image question count is zero or one, and any question appears after validation/handoff rather than before exploration.

trace_assertions.value: Do you want me to generate the HTML

trace_assertions.pattern: audit-overview|overview image|overview png|audit-overview\.png|总览图|概览图

evals.prompt: Use $repo-bug-audit at /path/to/repo-bug-audit to run a lightweight static Bug scan on one small Git repository and produce a concise handoff.
must_do: Treat the checkout as a single repo, not a repo-group parent.
Avoid forcing multi-repo shard machinery when the target is a single repo.
Require lens coverage unless submission-scope.md declares a narrowed lightweight/custom strategy.
Use --lens-scope custom only when the narrowed lens strategy is recorded.
must_not_do: Create a 20-repo style shard plan for a single small repository.
Skip lens coverage silently in a final handoff.
success_evidence: Final validation passes without requiring multi-repo shard artifacts.
Lens coverage exists, or the custom lightweight lens strategy is explicit in submission-scope.md.
No repo-group depth-complete claim appears.

evals.prompt: Use $repo-bug-audit at /path/to/repo-bug-audit to run a new deep audit on a target root that already contains an older bug-audit output package.
must_do: Treat the older package as a historical baseline for comparison only.
Run an independent current-source scan before reviewing the historical package.
Record the historical package as reviewed or excluded in submission-scope.md and depth-coverage.md.
Create current findings, repo profiles, indexes, and reports from the fresh scan.
must_not_do: Copy old Bug IDs, repo profiles, findings, indexes, report structure, or package counts into the new audit.
Use a prior package to justify skipping current repo exploration.
success_evidence: Depth coverage has a Historical Baselines section.
Historical baseline status is reviewed or excluded.
Validator exits 0 and does not flag imported, copied, or reused prior structure.
